article:
In brief: Today's weather in Abuja is warm and muggy, with temperatures feeling like 30°C due to the high humidity. As we look ahead to the rest of the week, we're expecting a mix of sunny days and potential rain showers, with temperatures fluctuating between the low 30s and mid-30s. If you're planning on visiting the National Assembly Complex or taking a stroll around the Millennium Park, make sure to pack accordingly and stay hydrated.

As of now, the current conditions in Abuja are quite warm, with a temperature of 26°C and a feels-like temperature of 30°C. The humidity is sitting at 81%, which is relatively high, but not uncommon for this time of year. The wind is blowing at a gentle 6 km/h, which should provide some relief from the heat. If you're planning on spending time outdoors, be sure to grab some water and take breaks in the shade, especially if you're going to be walking around the bustling Wuse Market.

The UV Index is currently at 0, which is great news if you're planning on spending time outside. However, don't let that fool you - the sun can still be intense, especially during the peak hours of the day. If you're driving around the city, you'll be happy to know that the traffic on the Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway is currently light, with speeds averaging around 37 km/h. This should make for a relatively smooth commute, whether you're heading to the Abuja National Mosque or the National Christian Centre.

As we take a look at the 7-day forecast, it's clear that we're in for a bit of a rollercoaster ride. [MAP: 7-day forecast map showing temperature and precipitation trends] Tomorrow, we're expecting a high of 33°C and a low of 26°C, with a 58% chance of rain. This could lead to some interesting road conditions, especially if the rain comes down heavily. If you're planning on driving to the nearby Zuma Rock, be sure to check the road conditions before you head out, as the roads can get slippery when wet.

### 2026-04-16
On Wednesday, we're expecting a high of 34°C and a low of 25°C, with only an 18% chance of rain. This should make for a great day to get outdoors and enjoy some of Abuja's many parks and green spaces. The [MAP: map of Abuja showing park locations] highlights some of the best spots to visit, including the beautiful Jabi Lake Park. With the sun shining and a gentle breeze, it's a great opportunity to take a boat ride or go for a walk around the lake.

However, it's worth noting that the heat can still be intense, even with the lower humidity. Make sure to pack plenty of water and sunscreen, and take breaks in the shade if you need to. The Aso Rock Presidential Villa is always a popular destination, but be sure to plan your visit accordingly, as the sun can reflect off the rocks and make the area feel even hotter.

### 2026-04-17
As we head into Thursday, things start to get a bit more interesting. We're expecting a high of 35°C and a low of 27°C, with a 49% chance of rain. This could lead to some thunderstorms in the afternoon, which may impact travel plans. If you're planning on driving to the nearby University of Abuja, be sure to check the road conditions before you head out, as the roads can get congested during heavy rain.

The [MAP: map of Abuja showing road conditions] highlights some of the areas that are most prone to flooding, including the Kubwa Expressway. It's always a good idea to plan your route in advance and leave a little extra time to get to your destination, especially during the rainy season.

### 2026-04-18
On Friday, we're expecting a bit of a cooldown, with a high of 31°C and a low of 26°C. There's still a 43% chance of rain, but the storms should be less intense than on Thursday. This could be a great day to visit some of Abuja's many museums, including the National Museum. The [MAP: map of Abuja showing museum locations] highlights some of the best spots to visit, including the Nigerian National Museum.

The museum is a great place to learn about the history and culture of Nigeria, and it's a nice air-conditioned spot to escape the heat. Plus, the surrounding area is quite walkable, with plenty of restaurants and cafes to grab a bite to eat.

### 2026-04-19
As we head into the weekend, things start to heat up again. On Saturday, we're expecting a high of 33°C and a low of 26°C, with a 55% chance of rain. This could make for some interesting conditions at the Abuja National Stadium, where there may be outdoor events scheduled. The [MAP: map of Abuja showing stadium location] highlights the area, which can get quite congested during events.

If you're planning on attending an event at the stadium, be sure to plan your transportation in advance and leave a little extra time to get to your seat. The traffic on the nearby roads can get heavy, especially during peak hours.

### 2026-04-20
On Sunday, we're expecting a high of 34°C and a low of 25°C, with only an 18% chance of rain. This should make for a great day to get outdoors and enjoy some of Abuja's many recreational activities, including golfing or hiking. The [MAP: map of Abuja showing recreational areas] highlights some of the best spots to visit, including the IBB Golf Club.

The club is a great place to spend the day, with beautifully manicured greens and a clubhouse with great food and drinks. Plus, the surrounding area is quite scenic, with plenty of opportunities to spot local wildlife.

### 2026-04-21
As we head into the new week, things are looking hot and dry. On Monday, we're expecting a high of 36°C and a low of 26°C, with only a 14% chance of rain. This could make for some challenging conditions, especially for those who have to spend time outdoors. The [MAP: map of Abuja showing heat index] highlights the areas that are most prone to heat stress, including the busy markets and outdoor workplaces.

It's always a good idea to take precautions when spending time outdoors in the heat, including drinking plenty of water and taking breaks in the shade. The heat can be intense, especially during the peak hours of the day, so be sure to plan your activities accordingly.
